page.title: Living Items Classification Workbench
page.description: First establish the classification system in read-only mode, then decide whether to add data entry and management later.

toast.deletePendingItem: {{name}} is queued for deletion. Undo is available for 5 seconds.
toast.deletePendingSystem: {{name}} is queued for deletion. Undo is available for 5 seconds.
toast.deletePendingSpace: {{name}} is queued for deletion. Undo is available for 5 seconds.
toast.deletePendingStage: {{name}} is queued for deletion. Undo is available for 5 seconds.

overview.currentFocusDesc: These aren't random thoughts, but reminders emerging from system gaps, action rhythm, and purchase judgment.
overview.noSpotlights: No system reminders to highlight under current filters.
overview.actionRhythmDesc: Every need has a different next step: restock, wait for a deal, buy once, or leave it alone.
overview.purchaseAlertsDesc: View depreciation, slow-buy items, and essentials together for quick switching.
overview.noHighDepreciationWarnings: No high-depreciation happiness items that need extra caution under current filters.
overview.noSlowDepreciationSuggestions: No slow-depreciation long-term item suggestions under current filters.
overview.noEssentialsReminder: No essentials reminder under current filter.
overview.stagePulseDescription: The configuration focus that best represents your current life stage.
overview.systemPulseDescription: Which item systems are forming real support surfaces.
overview.spacePulseDescription: Space scenes reveal the areas you use and maintain most frequently.
overview.heroTitle: Treat shopping as life structure, not a to-buy list
overview.heroDescription: The overview brings items, stages, systems, and spaces into one page so you can judge what needs filling, where density is too high, and what supports your current lifestyle.
overview.curatedSubtitle: Put supporting items, spotlights, and collections in the same field of view.
overview.focusReasonFallback: When there are no spotlight hints, this becomes the main narrative area for shopping strategy and attention points.
overview.lifestyleFallback: Grouping items that serve one lifestyle makes your real usage scenes visible faster.
overview.structureSubtitle: More items is not always better. The key is whether they form clear layers.
overview.structureChartHint: See quantity and organization cost together

system.answer: Which life function does it support?
system.detail: Systems like sleep, diet, cleaning, work/study help you see gaps first.
system.cue: Identify which life gap it fills before deciding if it needs immediate attention.

space.detail: Bedroom, desk, kitchen, entryway — inspect by physical space.
space.cue: Place it back in real space to more easily spot missing items and duplicates.

stage.answer: Does your current stage need it?
stage.detail: Moving essentials, renting, long-term living, home ownership — different depths.
stage.cue: Match items to your current living stage; don't hoard for the next stage too early.

necessity.answer: Would things fall apart without it?
necessity.detail: Minimum requirements and essentials determine priority; happiness items wait until foundations are stable.
necessity.cue: It helps you sort order, moving truly continuity-impacting items to the front.

lifecycle.answer: At what rhythm does it appear?
lifecycle.detail: Consumables need restocking, durables wait for deals, tools buy once, emotional items treat separately.
lifecycle.cue: All are needs, but restocking, waiting for deals, and one-time purchases lead to completely different next steps.

consumables.title: Restock Rhythm
consumables.detail: It gets used up. The point isn't whether it's worth it, but not letting inventory suddenly hit zero.

durables.title: Long-Term Use
durables.detail: It should accompany you for years. Focus on specs, frequency of use, and long-term return.

tools.title: Buy Once, Done
tools.detail: Low presence in daily life, but missing one will block you. Best to prepare in advance.

emotional.title: Treat Separately
emotional.detail: It doesn't exist by function alone, and shouldn't use the same replacement logic as other items.

attributes.label: Chinese Label
attributes.emptyHint: No attribute definitions in this category yet
attributes.selectPrompt: Select an attribute from the left to view details

hint.kind: Determines which dimension this attribute belongs to. Items use these as dropdown options.
hint.code: Use PascalCase, e.g. Consumable. Cannot be changed after creation.
hint.codeLocked: Code cannot be changed after creation — it is the stable database identifier.
hint.label: The label shown in the UI. Can be changed at any time.
hint.labelEn: Shown when the language is set to English. Falls back to the Chinese label if empty.
hint.semanticStatus: Built-in identifier for owned/wanted logic. Must match the system's business semantics. Locked for system attributes.
hint.semanticChannel: Channel attributes do not support semantic keys. Leave empty.
hint.semantic: Links this attribute to a built-in semantic (optional). Custom attributes usually leave this empty.
hint.styleToken: Badge background: accent=primary · secondary=secondary · muted=muted grey · card=card
hint.rank: Assign an order weight for ranked attributes (e.g. depreciation rate). Lower = lower rank. Leave empty for unordered attributes.
hint.description: Explains the meaning of this attribute. Only shown in the attribute management panel.

attributes.disableDescription: Some items are currently using this attribute.
attributes.disableKeepLegacy: Existing items will still be saved (old value passed through)
attributes.disableShowWarning: Disabled attributes will show a warning in the edit UI
attributes.disableBlockNew: Disabled attributes cannot be selected for new items
attributes.disabledWarning: This status is disabled. Consider selecting a different one.
attributes.conflict: This attribute was changed elsewhere — refreshed to the latest state, please retry.

error.itemRequired: Enter a name and select at least one system and one space
error.semanticRequired: This category requires a semantic key
error.codeFormat: Only letters, digits, and underscores are allowed, e.g. Consumable

validation.priceOrder: Buy-below price cannot be greater than overpay price
validation.invalidForm: The form has invalid values. Review the fields and try again.
validation.duplicateSystem: A stage template cannot contain the same system twice
validation.emptySection: Each system group needs at least one item
validation.duplicateName: That name already exists. Use a different one.

page.title: Belief Map
page.description: Classify beliefs with 4 dimensions, read impact distribution, and keep psychology as an optional lens.

classification.title: 4-D Belief Classification
classification.description: Group, filter, and map cognition by 4 dimensions; impact stays on each belief, not in the main filters.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Which part of life this belief acts on.

impact.title: Impact Direction
impact.description: Impact is not a fifth classification dimension; it belongs to each belief.

entries.title: Belief List
entries.description: Details always show impact and the optional psychology lens.

questions.title: Recurring Questions
questions.description: Keep the questions you repeatedly ask yourself.

relations.title: Relations
relations.description: Beliefs can be similar, opposed, or derived from each other.

cards.title: Three-Layer Frame
cards.description: A summary frame for worldview, life view, and values.

toast.deletePending: {{name}} is queued for deletion. Undo is available for 5 seconds.

error.required: Enter a title and belief statement

revision.autoSummary: Updated this belief in management mode.

empty.questions: No questions under the current filter.
empty.entries: No beliefs under the current filter.
empty.relations: No belief relations under the current filter.
empty.cards: No three-layer frame under the current filter.
empty.conflicts: No conflicting beliefs under the current filter.
empty.changing: No changing beliefs under the current filter.
empty.limiting: No limiting beliefs under the current filter.

page.title: Relationship States and Unfinished Words
page.description: Use five classification dimensions to see your relationship world, and unfinished weight to hold what has not been said yet.

overview.classificationDescription: These dimensions group and observe the relationship world; unfinished weight stays in details and unsent words.
overview.typeDescription: What is the core bond here?
overview.depthDescription: How open can I be in this relationship?
overview.stageDescription: Where is this relationship moving now?
overview.impactDescription: Is it nourishing, draining, neutral, or mixed?
overview.unfinishedDescription: Unfinished weight evaluates a single relationship and does not belong in the main filters.
overview.nourishingDetail: Relationships that restore or strengthen you
overview.drainingDetail: Relationships that need boundaries or fresh judgment
overview.repairingDetail: Relationships most worth gently checking now
overview.heavyDetail: Unfinished words to write down or process first

graph.helper: Circles connect people, people connect unsent words, and patterns add dashed links when their cues match tags or emotional signals.
graph.centeredHelper: Centered view prioritizes explicit person-to-person connections, then layers circle and pattern edges as supporting context.
graph.editHint: Switch to Control Mode to edit relationship, unsent-word, and pattern nodes directly from here.
graph.detailDescription: Select different node types to see their place, context, and nearby links in the relationship network.
graph.emptySelection: Select a node to see its details here.
graph.noPatternMatches: This pattern does not match any relationship nodes yet.

metrics.circlesDetail: How many relationship circles are in the current map
metrics.relationshipsDetail: Relationship people currently shown as graph nodes
metrics.notesDetail: Unsent-word nodes that are linked to relationship records
metrics.linksDetail: Connections between circles, people, notes, and patterns

empty.history: No depth or stage changes yet.
empty.graph: No relationship links to show yet.
empty.selectRelationship: Select a relationship from the left.
empty.selectNote: Select a note from the left.
empty.selectPattern: Select a pattern from the left.

edit.relationshipDescription: The five dimensions classify the relationship world; unfinished weight follows single relationships and unsent words.
edit.noteDescription: Write to an existing relationship, an independent target, or your future self.
edit.patternDescription: Review repeated roles and paths across relationships.
edit.name: Name
edit.connectionsDescription: Add real links between this person and others. When the other person opens edit mode, the roles will automatically flip into their perspective.
edit.noConnections: No person links yet. Add one like “student / teacher” or “friend / friend”.
edit.noEvents: No interaction events yet. Use Add above.
edit.noHistory: No change history yet. Use Add above.
edit.connectionRolesHint: Each row is a custom reciprocal relationship, for example current person as “student” and the other person as “teacher”; from the other side, the roles are shown reversed.
edit.connectionCardEmpty: No role pair yet. Open the card to fill in the details.
edit.connectionRoleNote: Add detail for this relationship row
edit.title: Title

page.title: Personal Nutrition Workbench
page.description: Recipe library + food classification + nutrition facts + daily plans + meal logs

overview.heroTitle: Today doesn't need to be perfect. It just needs to care for you.
overview.heroDescription: Bring recipes, foods, nutrition, and real meals into one page so you can see what to eat, what's missing, and what to do next.
overview.arrangedSlotsHint: Empty slots are not pressure, only planning signals
overview.foodsHint: The food library is the base layer for recipes and nutrition
overview.recipesHint: Only what you can make or want to recreate
overview.todayPlateDesc: Shape the plan into an executable day by meal slot.
overview.emptySlot: Not arranged yet, and that's allowed
overview.nutritionPulseDesc: A light estimate that never treats missing data as zero.
overview.completionCopy: There is already a plan today. The remaining work is making it easy to execute.
overview.profileDesc: Read the boundaries and current goal before arranging today's executable meals.

profileEdit.title: Edit Dietary Profile
profileEdit.description: The dietary profile records the current phase goal, hard boundaries, and soft stances that should shape daily plans.
profileEdit.intentNotePlaceholder: For example: observe the relationship between delivery food, caffeine, and sleep.
profileEdit.constraintsDescription: Hard constraints cover allergies, medical limits, or religious and cultural boundaries. Plans and suggestions should avoid them first.
profileEdit.constraintLabelPlaceholder: For example: reduce caffeine at night
profileEdit.stancesDescription: Soft stances are not bans. They are directions this phase should care about a little more.
profileEdit.stanceNotePlaceholder: How should it shape the plan?

validation.labelRequired: Complete the constraint or stance labels

dailyPlan.title: Daily Plan
dailyPlan.planNote: A plan is a fallback, not a check-in pressure
dailyPlan.emptySlot: Leave this meal blank for now
dailyPlan.emptySlotsHint: They can stay blank, or become a low-friction fallback plan
dailyPlan.pendingNutritionHint: Missing data is never counted as zero
dailyPlan.replacementHint: Suggested from unplanned recipes with more complete nutrition data.
dailyPlan.noReplacement: No replacement recipes yet. Add recipes or complete nutrition data first.
dailyPlan.missingNutrition: {{count}} entries have pending nutrition data and were not counted as zero.

dailyPlanEdit.description: Daily plans can reference recipes, foods, or temporary text. Empty meal slots can stay blank.
dailyPlanEdit.slotsHint: Each meal slot can stay empty or combine multiple planned entries.
dailyPlanEdit.deleteBlocked: This plan has {{count}} linked meal logs and cannot be deleted yet.

validation.slotRequired: Keep at least one meal slot

recipes.title: Recipes
recipes.search: Search recipes, tags, or meal slots
recipes.recentFeedbackNoNote: This log has no extra note.
recipes.noRecentFeedback: No real meal feedback yet. It will appear here after you generate a log from a plan or add one manually.

recipeEdit.description: Recipes reference foods from the food library, and nutrition is derived from ingredients.
recipeEdit.name: Recipe Name
recipeEdit.summaryPlaceholder: What situation is this recipe good for?
recipeEdit.missingFoodMemory: Linked food memory no longer exists
recipeEdit.ingredientsHint: Ingredients come from the food library; nutrition is derived by amount.
recipeEdit.deleteBlocked: This recipe is referenced by {{count}} plans or logs and cannot be deleted yet.

validation.mealRoleRequired: Select at least one meal role
validation.ingredientsRequired: Add at least one valid ingredient

foods.title: Food Classification
foods.allFoodsDesc: Browse and search the whole food library across categories.
foods.noNote: No note yet. Storage, usage, and constraints can be added later.
foods.noDietaryTags: No dietary tags or allergens yet.
foods.profileMissingCopy: This food can still be used in recipes and plans, but the nutrition table will show it as pending.

categoryEdit.description: Food categories organize the food library and shape list views, filters, and nutrition table reading paths.
categoryEdit.name: Category Name
categoryEdit.descriptionPlaceholder: How does this category help you choose foods?
categoryEdit.deleteBlocked: This category is referenced by {{count}} foods and cannot be deleted yet.

foodEdit.description: Foods are the base data source for recipes, daily plans, and nutrition facts.
foodEdit.name: Food Name
foodEdit.namePlaceholder: For example: egg, tomato, brown rice
foodEdit.nutritionDescription: Maintain values per 100g / 100ml. Blank fields are shown as pending, not counted as zero.
foodEdit.deleteBlocked: This food is referenced by {{count}} recipes, plans, or logs and cannot be deleted yet.

nutrients.title: Nutrition Facts
nutrients.emptyHint: Clear search, switch categories, or turn off the pending-only filter.
nutrients.drawerDescription: This detail view currently combines available nutrient fields with stable simulated scores; real targets can be connected later.
nutrients.chartHint: Like a game stat radar: farther out means stronger presence; missing fields are temporarily simulated.

logs.title: Meal Logs
logs.description: Record what actually happened so the plan can be calibrated.
logs.search: Search meals, scenes, feedback, or food memories
logs.boundary: This keeps the observation clues: why you ate, how it felt afterward, and why the plan changed. It calibrates plans, not judges you.

logEdit.description: Meal logs help you review what actually happened and can link back to a planned meal slot.
logEdit.missingPlannedSlot: Linked plan slot no longer exists
logEdit.missingFoodMemory: Linked food memory no longer exists
logEdit.entriesHint: Record recipes, individual foods, or temporary unstructured text.
logEdit.textTitlePlaceholder: For example: a delivery set meal
logEdit.changeReasonPlaceholder: If it differed from the plan, what happened?
logEdit.notePlaceholder: Body feeling, mood, context, or follow-up adjustment

validation.entryRequired: Add at least one meal entry

page.title: See the whole picture before choosing where to begin

recent.title: Recent Records
recent.description: Start by seeing yourself through recent events and recent words.

logic.title: Page Logic
logic.description: Make the reason for this workspace structure clear first.

pulse.title: Today’s Observations
pulse.description: Give yourself an honest enough starting point.

quick.title: Quick Entry
quick.description: Start with action, then decide whether to open it further.

record.title: Recording Workbench
record.detail: Reflection, events, finance, and living items capture how you are actually living right now.

inner.title: Inner State
inner.detail: The emotions page carries recent psychological waves, triggers, and recovery methods that work for you.

selfMap.title: Self Map
selfMap.detail: Beliefs, principles, relationships, growth, and memories explain why you have become this version of yourself.

legacyFuture.title: Legacy and Future
legacyFuture.detail: Legacy organizes important words and entrusted matters, while the future blueprint brings ideal self and stage paths forward.

reflection.label: Write a reflection
reflection.description: Put down the thoughts and feelings of this moment first.

events.label: Record an event
events.description: Add a timeline fragment and keep a recent life detail.

finance.label: Record spending
finance.description: Make real choices and lifestyle evidence visible.

emotion.label: View emotion waves
emotion.description: See recent inner weather and triggers together.

future.label: Clarify future direction
future.description: Make ideal self and lifestyle clearer.

beliefs.label: Open belief map
beliefs.description: See how your worldview and values currently look.

relationships.label: Review relationships
relationships.description: Put important people, patterns, and unsaid words in one place.

journey.label: Review growth memories
journey.description: Reconnect life stages, memory nodes, places, and objects.

legacy.label: Organize entrusted words
legacy.description: Place words, concerns, and instructions for the future.

currentFocus.title: Current Focus
currentFocus.description: Bring the new modules onto the same desk first, so they can be viewed, switched, and grounded in basic data.

empty.recent: No recent records match the current filter.

page.title: Write down this moment first
page.description: The expression does not need to be complete. Honest is better than polished. This is the most natural place to start.

draft.title: Writing Start
draft.description: Keep a concrete example nearby. Saved reflections live in the record list on the right.

recent.title: Recent Reflections
recent.description: Review what you have written recently, then decide how to organize it later.

themes.title: Theme Threads
themes.description: View tags and months together to see which topics keep returning.

writingPrompts.title: Review Questions
writingPrompts.description: You do not need complete answers before writing; use questions to return attention to yourself.

placeholders.title: For example: what I really want to keep today
placeholders.excerpt: What happened today? What did I notice? What should future review remember?
placeholders.tags: Separate with commas, for example: rhythm, work

empty.records: No reflections match the current filter.
empty.tags: No tags under the current filter.
empty.months: No month distribution under the current filter.

edit.description: Keep the time, title, short body, and a few tags.

validation.required: Enter the time, title, and reflection

prompts.keep: What judgment from this moment do I want to keep long term?
prompts.pattern: Has this appeared in recent reflections more than once?
prompts.next: If I only make one small adjustment, what comes next?

page.title: Reconnect life fragments to the timeline
page.description: Capture events, experiences, fragments, and thoughts so later review can recover what actually happened.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.description: Review recent fragments by when they happened.

capture.title: Quick Capture
capture.description: Keep the time, title, and short note first.

themes.title: Recent Themes
themes.description: Repeated themes make the shape of daily life easier to see.

empty.timeline: No events match the current filter.
empty.themes: No themes match the current filter.
empty.review: No theme cues to review yet.

edit.description: Events keep only the necessary signals: time, title, short note, and theme.

placeholders.title: For example: talked with a friend about future cities
placeholders.excerpt: What happened? Which detail would be useful to review later?
placeholders.tags: Separate with commas, for example: city, future

validation.required: Enter a time, title, and short note

review.title: Review Cues
review.description: Turn recurring themes into questions for the next review.
review.themeLine: {{theme}} has appeared {{count}} times; review the life pattern behind it.

page.title: Turn resource flow into evidence
page.description: Look at income, expenses, categories, and review status together to see what kind of life your money is shaping.

sections.entriesDescription: Income and expenses stay on the same real-life timeline.
sections.distributionDescription: See where money goes first, then what part of life it supports.
sections.reviewDescription: Targets are not pressure. They give money a direction.

target.empty: There is no target for the current month yet.

empty.entries: No finance entries under the current filter.
empty.distribution: No distribution data under the current filter.
empty.reviewEntries: There are no priority entries to review right now.

edit.description: Capture the amount, and also the life context behind it.

validation.required: Please fill in date, title, category, and a valid amount

rules.title: Budget Rules
rules.description: Calibrate each category’s intent and limit in one place.

page.title: Classify the past, then see how it still shapes the present
page.description: Five dimensions organize, filter, and review memory. Formative power stays attached to individual memories and growth nodes instead of becoming a primary filter.

type.title: Type
type.description: Start with the form this memory is carried by.

era.title: Era
era.description: Eras can be multi-select. Primary era is only for timeline placement.

emotionalWeight.title: Emotional Weight
emotionalWeight.description: How heavy does it feel to touch now?

processing.title: Processing
processing.description: Not every part of the past needs to be fully processed.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Sensitive memories need clear boundaries before review.

classification.title: 5-D Overview
classification.description: These dimensions belong in filtering and grouping. Formative power stays on the timeline and in detail.

formativePower.title: Formative Power
formativePower.description: Formative power explains depth of influence and is not a primary filter.

timeline.title: Memory Timeline
timeline.description: Review memories by primary era while always keeping formative power visible.

growth.title: Growth Nodes
growth.description: When multiple memories point in the same direction of change, they form a growth node.

anchors.title: Sensory Anchors
anchors.description: Many memories do not start with events. They begin with places, objects, or photos.

reviewPrompts.title: Review Prompts
reviewPrompts.description: These prompts help you approach the past gradually instead of forcing a full retelling.

threads.title: Influences Still Active Now
threads.description: Signals surfacing from growth nodes that are worth cross-checking with beliefs, principles, and relationships.

control.title: Structure Review
control.description: Control mode can maintain memories, growth nodes, anchors, prompts, and active signals while also spotting missing fields and broken links.
control.missingLinksTitle: Memory IDs referenced by growth nodes but not found in the current dataset

edit.listHint: One per line, or separate with commas.
edit.sourceModulesHint: Only recognized source module names are saved.
edit.memoryIdsHint: One ID per line, or separate with commas; invalid IDs are ignored.
edit.memoryDescription: Maintain a memory's classification, summary, impact, and review signals.
edit.growthDescription: Use memory IDs to connect before, after, and trigger memories.
edit.anchorDescription: Connect places, objects, people, or photos with memories.
edit.promptDescription: Record a question that can be revisited slowly.
edit.threadDescription: Record an influence that is still active and worth reviewing across modules.

validation.memoryRequired: Enter a title, primary era, summary, and impact
validation.growthRequired: Enter title, before/after states, key event, and a valid trigger memory
validation.anchorRequired: Enter the anchor name and note

empty.memories: No memories match the current filters.
empty.growthNodes: No growth nodes match the current filters.
empty.anchors: No memory anchors match the current filters.
empty.reviewPrompts: No review prompts match the current filters.
empty.threads: No active influence signals match the current filters.
empty.signals: No supporting signals match the current filters.

page.title: Map how the external economy works
page.description: Classify knowledge with five dimensions, read relevance as decision distance, and use the heatmap to spot blind spots.

classification.title: 5-Dimensional Cognition Map
classification.description: Classify by five dimensions; relevance stays attached to each item instead of becoming a main filter.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Which part of economic operation this belongs to.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: From recognizing the term to having your own framework.

discipline.title: Discipline
discipline.description: Separate economics from sociology before drilling into details.

relevance.title: Decision Distance
relevance.description: Relevance is not a main filter; it shows whether this knowledge is useful for current decisions.

heatmap.title: Domain × Confidence
heatmap.stackedDescription: See where a domain is genuinely understood and where it is only familiar as a term.

entries.title: Knowledge List
entries.description: Each item always shows confidence and decision distance.

gaps.title: Knowledge Gaps
gaps.description: Name the areas that are still only familiar or not yet framed.

prompts.title: Review Prompts
prompts.description: Use these as a monthly check-in for the cognition map.

review.title: Priority Repairs
review.description: Short decision distance plus shallow confidence is the first place to study.

empty.entries: No knowledge entries match the current filters.
empty.topicArea: No entries match {{topic}} under the current filters.
empty.gaps: No knowledge gaps match the current filters.
empty.prompts: No review prompts match the current filters.
empty.review: No priority study items match the current filters.
empty.economics: No economics entries match the current filters.
empty.sociology: No sociology entries match the current filters.

edit.entryDescription: Each entry needs domain, layer, confidence, source, and decision distance.
edit.historyPlaceholder: 2026-03-04 | 听过名词 -> 知道大致逻辑 | Finished a reading set

validation.entryRequired: Please enter a title and summary
validation.history: Revision history must use: date | old level -> new level | trigger
validation.sourceRefs: Source format must be: source label | https://example.com
validation.gapRequired: Please enter the gap and next step

economics.title: Economics
economics.description: Organize decision-relevant knowledge about prices, markets, policy, firms, and macro mechanisms.

sociology.title: Sociology
sociology.description: Understand how people act inside groups and institutions through structure, mobility, organizations, cities, and norms.

discipline.mapDescription: See where this discipline already has a framework and where it is still just a familiar term.

graph.title: Knowledge Links
graph.description: Put economics and sociology on the same relationship map to see how concepts explain one another.
graph.helper: Drag nodes to re-read the structure, click a node for details, and click empty space to clear selection.
graph.detailDescription: The card on the right follows the selected node and translates the map position back into readable context.
graph.emptySelection: Select a node to see its details here.
graph.conceptDescription: This concept works like a junction, pulling scattered knowledge entries back into one shared map.

metrics.entriesDetail: Knowledge entries that already point to related concepts
metrics.nodesDetail: Knowledge nodes plus concept nodes together
metrics.linksDetail: Connections that point from entries to concepts

page.title: Read the recent inner weather
page.description: See emotional waves, triggers, and recovery methods in one place.

today.title: Today’s Emotion Entry
today.description: Intensity, body signals, and immediate impulses.
today.longDescription: Turn vague emotion into a sample you can revisit.
today.promptDescription: Intensity, tags, triggers, and needs all feed later pattern observation.

prompt.feelingBody: It does not need to be exact; pick the closest word.
prompt.bodyBody: Shoulders, chest, appetite, and sleepiness often know before language does.
prompt.needBody: Not to fix it immediately, but to know what would feel safer now.

timeline.title: Emotion Timeline
timeline.description: Read peaks, dips, and recovery periods through time.
timeline.loopDescription: See rhythms that are not random.

triggers.title: Triggers
triggers.description: Connect emotion with concrete life events.
triggers.lifestyleDesc: Sleep, food, movement, and screen time.
triggers.environmentDesc: Contexts that tend to worsen state.
triggers.relationshipsDesc: Relationships that tend to create more fluctuation.

toolbox.title: Soothing & Recovery Toolkit
toolbox.description: When things are hard, stabilize first and analyze later.
toolbox.minimalSteps: If things are already bad, only do these three
toolbox.ineffectiveDesc: Avoid these paths when your state is low.
toolbox.recoveryNotesDesc: Past effective recovery experiences can be used again.

empty.trend: No emotion samples to chart yet.
empty.loopPatterns: No recurring loop has been identified yet.
empty.triggers: No triggers under the current filter.
empty.tools: No recovery tools under the current filter.

editor.description: This content is saved to backend emotion data instead of temporary page mock data.

overview.title: Edit Emotion Overview
overview.description: Maintain the recent window, intensity, and one-line summary.

textList.description: One item per line; saved back to the backend.

placeholders.list: One per line, or separate with commas

page.title: Name the person you want to become
page.description: The future here is not a prediction. It is the direction you choose to write down: ideal self, ideal life, values, and stage paths.

definition.title: Ideal Definition
definition.description: Clarify the direction first, then make the path concrete.

milestones.title: Stage Paths
milestones.description: Break the future into ways to move closer, not a final answer.

experiments.title: Current Experiments
experiments.description: No need to arrive all at once. Let daily life become a little more like you.

metrics.valuesDetail: Values are not slogans. They are ongoing filters.
metrics.milestonesDetail: Turn distant direction into nearby steps.
metrics.experimentsDetail: Let small changes appear in real life first.

definition.valuesDesc: These words decide where your time and money go.

edit.blueprintDescription: Save the core definition here: who you want to become, the life you want, and the values that matter.
edit.identityPlaceholder: Write the self-state you want to move closer to.
edit.lifestylePlaceholder: Write about space, work rhythm, relationships, and recovery.
edit.valuesHint: One per line. Commas also work.
edit.milestoneDescription: Turn distant direction into a path you can approach.
edit.experimentDescription: Experiments do not need to be grand. They only need to move life closer to the blueprint.

validation.blueprintRequired: Please fill in ideal self and ideal life
validation.milestoneRequired: Please fill in horizon, summary, and at least one step

empty.milestones: No future paths match the current filter.
empty.experiments: No experiments match the current filter.
empty.identity: No ideal self has been written yet.

alignment.title: Alignment Check
alignment.description: Use four questions to check whether the blueprint returns to present action.

path.title: Is the path close enough?
path.empty: No milestone is ready to carry the current direction yet.

experiment.title: Is there an experiment in daily life?

legacy.pageTitle: Place the important words before they become urgent
legacy.pageDescription: The item library is the only editing source; overview, delivery map, relationship expression, and trust boundaries are derived views.

toast.deletePendingItem: Queued for deletion: {{title}}. You can undo within 5 seconds.
toast.deleteFailedItem: Failed to delete life organizing item

metrics.totalDesc: The item library is the only data source.
metrics.criticalDraftDesc: Check what is still draft or continuously updated.
metrics.missingDeliveryDesc: Future or conditional visibility needs a trigger description.
metrics.finalLockedDesc: Locked content needs deliberate confirmation before edits.
metrics.heavyLoadDesc: Best opened when there is enough steadiness.
metrics.aiExcludedDesc: These items stay out of cross-module insight.
metrics.secondConfirmDesc: A gentle pause remains before opening.

overview.title: Life Organizing Overview
overview.description: See coverage, gaps, and boundaries without turning heavy content into pressure.
overview.coverageDesc: Check whether the work is overly concentrated in one category.
overview.boundaryDesc: Only counts and cues are shown here; private content is not auto-summarized.
overview.recentDesc: Only summaries and boundaries are shown, not full content.
overview.emotionalLoadDesc: Emotional load is not a main category; it is an opening cue per item.
overview.reviewPromptsDesc: Organize slowly without turning this into completion pressure.
overview.missingDeliveryCopy: These can remain drafts, but final versions need clear trigger and verification wording.

items.title: Item Library
items.selectPrompt: Select an item to view details.
items.secondConfirmCopy: This item requires a second confirmation, so only its summary is shown first.

edit.lockedCopy: This item is locked. Unlock it before editing.

validation.required: Please fill in title, summary, and content
validation.criticalFinalDelivery: Critical final items need a delivery condition

deliveryMap.title: Delivery Map
deliveryMap.description: Check who sees what by recipient and visibility.
deliveryMap.groupDescription: V1 records delivery intent and manual confirmation conditions only. It does not promise automatic delivery.
deliveryMap.checklistDesc: These checks come from item fields and do not create separate data.

relationship.title: Relationship Expression
relationship.description: Focus on words for someone and unfinished concerns tied to relationships.
relationship.now: Words that can be said now
relationship.nowDesc: These may move into the relationship module later, but nothing is pushed automatically.
relationship.future: Words for future or after death
relationship.futureDesc: Keep delivery intent, recipient, and condition beside the text.
relationship.privateDesc: These should not be pushed automatically or enter AI summaries by default.
relationship.unlinkedDesc: You can decide later whether to reference a relationship record.
relationship.nowHint: visibility=now is only a cue that expression is possible; it is not a nudge or automatic copy.

boundaries.title: Trust Boundaries
boundaries.description: Make system boundaries explicit and gather item-level protection rules in one view.
boundaries.rulesDesc: These notes come from backend configuration and are maintained separately from item rules.
boundaries.legalCopy: Life Organizing has no legal will effect and does not replace insurance, asset, medical, or legal document management.

empty.filteredItems: No life organizing items match the current filters.

shell.searchPlaceholder: Search records, beliefs, relationships, or growth clues
shell.apiErrorFallback: API request failed, page has fallen back to local mock data.

themes.title: Theme
themes.description: A theme is a work atmosphere, not a day/night mode. Current theme: {{theme}}.

morning.label: Morning
morning.description: A calm blue-gray base for organizing and reviewing.

linen.label: Paper
linen.description: A warmer paper and clay palette for a slower workbench.

tide.label: Tide
tide.description: A lighter, cooler palette for stepping back and seeing the structure.

music.title: Music
music.description: Part of the persistent utility area. It offers light company without taking over the page.
music.unsupported: Realtime audio is not supported in this environment. The UI can stay and later be replaced with a real player.
music.volumeHint: Keep the utility area light so it does not cover the main page.

morning.label: Morning
morning.description: A brighter, lighter melody for organizing thoughts.

rain.label: Rain
rain.description: A slower, lower pattern for staying with the work longer.

night.label: Night Reading
night.description: A more distant tone for calm evening work.

notificationCenter.title: Notification Center
notificationCenter.description: Top messages and bottom notifications are collected here for review and follow-up.
notificationCenter.empty: No notifications yet. Save results, reminders, and cross-page follow-ups can be wired here later.

message.title: Top message triggered
message.message: It disappears after 3 seconds by default and suits lightweight feedback like save success.
message.detail: Top messages work best for brief confirmations and lightweight result feedback, not heavy reading.
message.lineUse: Best for save success, completion confirmations, and light reminders
message.lineShape: Keep the message short and actions minimal

notification.title: Bottom notification triggered
notification.message: It stays for 5 seconds by default and suits fuller information prompts.
notification.detail: Bottom notifications fit contextual reminders, suggestions, and actionable information, especially when stacked.
notification.lineUse: Best for task completion, reminders, and suggestions
notification.lineShape: Priority, source, and category can be extended later

persistent.title: Life timeline needs more context
persistent.message: This notification stays until you handle or close it.
persistent.detail: Persistent notifications suit items that truly need attention, such as missing content, confirmations, or cross-page reminders.
persistent.lineTarget: Can navigate to a specific page
persistent.lineShape: Can later become a task, todo, or message-center entry

legacy.label: Life Organizing
legacy.hint: Important handoffs and words for the future

socioeconomics.label: Socioeconomics
socioeconomics.hint: A cognition map of the external economy

capture.title: Capture First
capture.body: Capture first, then organize, then understand why you think and live this way.

review.title: Review Gently
review.body: Do not rush to a conclusion. First look at recent emotions, choices, and rhythm.

future.title: Leave Room for Future
future.body: Make the ideal life more concrete, and direction can slowly become action.

rhythmPopup.title: Slow down and look at today first

explore.label: Explore
explore.text: Trace the causal evolution of four ancient civilizations through a geopolitical materialist lens.

fit.label: Best For
fit.text: Useful when you want to understand civilizational choices and institutional diversity from first-layer geographic constraints.

purpose.label: Purpose
purpose.text: The goal is not to memorize history, but to see how the same survival pressure can produce different civilizational paths.

about.description: A local-first personal life management system

page.title: Organize principles, boundaries, and bottom lines into a decision system
page.description: This page does more than list principles: it shows what each one protects, where it came from, how firm it is, and what it costs to keep.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Which part of life it mainly protects.

type.title: Type
type.description: Whether it is a bottom line, boundary, or standard.

classification.title: 5-Dimension Classification
classification.description: These dimensions organize and review the decision system; cost stays in details and calibration.

entries.title: Principle List
entries.description: Cost is always visible in details, so the real price of each principle stays in view.

boundaries.title: Where I No Longer Yield
boundaries.description: Bottom lines are not performance; they mark what cannot keep being crossed.

prompts.title: Decision Calibration
prompts.description: For a concrete choice, pull up the related principles first, then check strength and cost.

relations.title: Support And Conflict
relations.description: Principles are not isolated dots; they can support each other or pull in different directions.

cost.title: Cost Annotation
cost.description: Cost is an evaluation attribute for a single principle. It shows what you need to prepare to keep it, and stays out of the main filters.

empty.entries: No principles match the current filter.
empty.boundaries: No bottom lines match the current filter.
empty.prompts: No decision prompts match the current filter.
empty.relations: No principle relations match the current filter.
empty.priority: No high-priority principles match the current filter.
empty.evolving: No changing principles match the current filter.
empty.personal: No personal principles match the current filter.
empty.others: No borrowed or inherited principles match the current filter.

edit.description: The 5 classification dimensions organize the decision system; cost evaluates the price of keeping one principle.
edit.title: Title
edit.titlePlaceholder: Example: Avoid important decisions late at night
edit.statementPlaceholder: Write the principle as one action-guiding sentence.

validation.required: Please fill in title, statement, and boundary

personal.title: Personal Principles
personal.description: View the principles you derived, confirmed through experience, and actually want to keep living by.

others.title: Others' Principles
others.description: Separate inherited, observed, or temporarily borrowed principles so you can decide which ones are truly yours.

perspective.summaryDescription: See whether this set is becoming internalized, already stable, or still borrowed from others.
perspective.signalDescription: Start with the non-negotiable or high-cost principles and check whether they still genuinely belong to you.

worldhistory.title: Human Civilization Geo-Causal Star Map
worldhistory.subtitle: Cross-cultural comparison from a geo-materialist perspective

narrative.start: Walk through causal chain step by step

preset.noPresets: This pair of civilizations has no verdicts

arena.title: Cross-Civilization Dialectical Arena
arena.needsTwo: At least two civilizations needed for comparison
